title: Claude Fable 5 + YouTube = $30,000/mo
summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates a YouTube channel automation pipeline using Claude Code Fable 5 for video editing, Higsfield CLI for image handling, and Auphonic for audio processing. The workflow involves deconstructing successful channels, writing scripts, recording voice, and automating video assembly at roughly $2 per video.
writeup: Jack Roberts walks through a YouTube channel automation pipeline built on Claude Code Fable 5 for video editing, Higsfield CLI for image handling, and Auphonic for audio processing. The workflow deconstructs successful channels, writes scripts, records voice, and automates video assembly at roughly $2 per video. The framing ties these steps to a stated $30,000 per month target. The takeaway is a concrete tool stack and a low per-video cost for assembling content programmatically.
